prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime unit sprite for a tactics game
Primary request: create a revised Myrmidons Spectre as a single full-body transparent-background sprite that preserves the vat-grown mutant identity from reference image 1 and the approved Myrmidon faction rendering language from reference image 2, but remove the ammo belt entirely
Subject: an eerie amphibious bio-engineered humanoid with aquatic features, elongated claws, biotech tubes along the shoulder and back, lean muscular build, and dark field trousers; no chest bandolier, no ammo belt, no bullet strap, and no ammunition anywhere on the body
Style/medium: painterly pixel-art-inspired 2D game sprite, moody post-apocalyptic tactics art
Composition/framing: centered full-body grounded stance, tight bounds, readable when rendered around 100 px tall on the battlefield
Lighting/mood: restrained cool underwater-biotech mood, readable silhouette, not transparent
Color palette: sea-green skin, teal bioluminescent nodes, dark slate trousers, muted organic tubing, restrained rust-brown only on fittings
Constraints: transparent background; exactly one subject; preserve the vat-grown aquatic mutant silhouette and biotech tube language; remove all ammo-belt and bandolier shapes; body should read solid and opaque; alpha only outside the character silhouette; no tank, no machinery, no floor, no scenery, no text, no radial vignette
Avoid: extra characters, rifles, pistols, ammunition, military chest strap, bulky armor, anime rendering, glossy 3D, halo glow, cropped feet, merged hands, extra limbs
